power_integrity.desc: All power and ground rails from the official pinlist must be connected.

config_pins.desc: Dedicated configuration pins must be reviewed and terminated against the selected boot mode.

diff_pair_integrity.desc: Differential pairs from the official pinlist must be routed as complete P/N pairs.

notes: Official product page provides user IO count, but the current local raw bundle does not contain a package-bank map for this package.

source_conflicts.note: Official pinlist row count does not match the package suffix pin count; review NC/DNU handling before freezing library symbols.

notes: UG915 confirms DDR3/DDR4 support for PH1A90/PH1A180/PH1A400 families; exact package-bank ownership should still be frozen in TD IO planning.
Current Chinese product page lists MIPI IO on this package, but the local raw hardware guide does not yet cover this package name.
UG909 documents bidirectional adjacent-DUAL refclk sharing on PH1A90/PH1A180.
Current local PCIe manual covers PH1A90SEG324 but not this package name; do not freeze PCIe ownership without package-specific confirmation.
Official locale pages disagree on some package-level parameters for this device.

SGMII.note: 1G serial Ethernet PHY-side links commonly use a 125 MHz differential reference clock.

XAUI.note: 10G serial Ethernet families commonly use a 156.25 MHz differential reference clock.

refclk_requirements.selection_note: Refclk frequency, clock standard, jitter budget, and pair-to-quad mapping must be frozen against the selected protocol before schematic sign-off.

lane_group_mappings.selection_note: Freeze the protocol/IP assignment for this lane group and bind it to one of the mapped refclk pairs before schematic sign-off.
Freeze the protocol/IP assignment for this lane group and bind it to one of the mapped refclk pairs before schematic sign-off.

memory_interface_review.selection_note: Freeze DDR bank ownership, byte-group placement, and VREF strategy against the exact package before schematic sign-off.

source_consistency_review.selection_note: Package-level facts disagree across official locales; do not freeze board assumptions until the exact package capability is re-confirmed.
